thread.title: Kejriwal Claims AAP's Victory Marks 'Semi-Finals' for Upcoming Delhi and Punjab Elections | Chandigarh News - Times of India
thread.title_full: Kejriwal Claims AAP's Victory Marks 'Semi-Finals' for Upcoming Delhi and Punjab Elections | Chandigarh News - Times of India

author: TNN
title: Kejriwal Claims AAP's Victory Marks 'Semi-Finals' for Upcoming Delhi and Punjab Elections | Chandigarh News - Times of India
text: Chandigarh: Terming the four Assembly byelections as semi-finals for 2025 Delhi and 2027 Punjab polls, AAP supremo Arvind Kejriwal said AAP’s win on three seats marks a new milestone, as these seats had never been won by AAP before—not even during the 2022 wave that saw the party take 92 out of 117 seats in the Punjab Assembly.Addressing party workers at AAP headquarters in Delhi along with CM Bhagwant Mann and other party leaders, “The most significant aspect is that the BJP lost its deposits on all three seats. The way the Aam Aadmi Party has made its mark in the country’s politics is remarkable.” Kejriwal said these results showed that the govt must be doing good work. “Otherwise, why would people support us so strongly again? I would call this the semi-final for the 2027 elections. By giving us three out of four seats today, the people of Punjab have said, “You’re doing a great job, keep it up. If you continue this way, we’ll give you even more than 92 seats in 2027.” TNNWe also published the following articles recentlyIn run up to Delhi polls, Arvind Kejriwal urges AAP workers to be ready for dharamyudhAam Aadmi Party leader Arvind Kejriwal has likened the upcoming Delhi assembly elections to a righteous battle, invoking religious imagery to rally his party workers. He highlighted AAP's achievements while criticizing the BJP's lack of action despite its resources. Meanwhile, the Delhi BJP has begun its election preparations, forming committees and strategizing for the 2025 polls.115410499Punjab bypoll results: AAP wins 3 seats, loses from its stronghold to CongressPunjab's ruling AAP won three out of four assembly by-elections, solidifying its position in the state. The victories in Chabbewal, Dera Baba Nanak, and Gidderbaha were seen as a setback for Congress, particularly for MPs whose family members contested and lost. AAP's success in Gidderbaha, a Congress stronghold, was a significant win, signaling public approval of the party's governance.115594362AAP govt to set up new model of law and order in Punjab: Arvind KejriwalDuring an event for new police recruits, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal praised the Punjab Police and pledged to establish a new model for law and order in the state. Kejriwal highlighted the AAP government's success in Delhi's education, health, and power sectors, aiming to replicate this success in Punjab.115372724
